rationale: This structure is organized around established clinical specialties, mirroring how medical professionals and literature are segmented. It balances organ-system-based fields (e.g., Cardiology, Neurology), population-based fields (e.g., Pediatrics), and modality-based specialties (e.g., Surgery, Radiology). We deliberately kept 'Surgery' as a single, broad category to avoid excessive fragmentation into sub-disciplines like orthopedics or neurosurgery, which would make the list too granular. Conversely, we split 'Internal Medicine' into its core subspecialties (Gastroenterology, Pulmonology, etc.) because each possesses a distinct professional identity, body of literature, and audience, making a single 'Internal Medicine' category too coarse.

subspecialties.name: Oncology
subspecialties.tagline: The study and treatment of cancer, from chemotherapy to immunotherapy.
covers: Medical, radiation, and surgical oncology
Cancer biology, diagnostics, and novel therapeutics

subspecialties.name: Cardiology
subspecialties.tagline: The heart and blood vessels, from hypertension to heart failure.
covers: Ischemic heart disease, arrhythmias, structural heart disease, vascular medicine
Interventional cardiology, electrophysiology, cardiac imaging

subspecialties.name: Neurology
subspecialties.tagline: Disorders of the nervous system, from stroke to neurodegeneration.
covers: Stroke, epilepsy, multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's, Alzheimer's
Headache, neuromuscular disorders, clinical neurophysiology

subspecialties.name: Surgery
subspecialties.tagline: The art and science of operative treatment and procedures.
covers: General surgery, orthopedics, trauma, cardiothoracic, neurosurgery
Surgical techniques, perioperative care, clinical outcomes
explicitly_excludes: Non-operative management of surgical conditions → relevant medical specialty

subspecialties.name: Infectious Diseases
subspecialties.tagline: From pandemics to hospital-acquired infections and antimicrobial stewardship.
covers: Virology, bacteriology, mycology in a clinical context
Vaccines, antibiotics, HIV/AIDS, sepsis, global health infections

subspecialties.name: Radiology & Medical Imaging
subspecialties.tagline: Visualizing the human body for diagnosis and intervention.
covers: Diagnostic imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI, Ultrasound)
Interventional radiology, nuclear medicine, imaging physics
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of tissue slides → Pathology & Laboratory Medicine

subspecialties.name: Gastroenterology & Hepatology
subspecialties.tagline: The digestive system, from the esophagus to the liver and bowel.
covers: IBD, liver disease, endoscopy, GI motility, pancreatic disorders
Hepatitis, GI cancers, nutritional science
explicitly_excludes: Surgical treatment of GI diseases → Surgery

subspecialties.name: Pulmonology & Critical Care
subspecialties.tagline: Lungs, airways, and the management of critically ill patients.
covers: Asthma, COPD, interstitial lung disease, sleep medicine
Mechanical ventilation, sepsis, intensive care unit (ICU) management
explicitly_excludes: Anesthesia for critical care → Anesthesiology & Pain Medicine

subspecialties.name: Pediatrics
subspecialties.tagline: Medical care for infants, children, and adolescents.
covers: General pediatrics, neonatology, developmental disorders, pediatric subspecialties
Childhood diseases, vaccination, adolescent medicine
explicitly_excludes: Adult presentations of disease → specific adult specialty

subspecialties.name: Obstetrics & Gynecology
subspecialties.tagline: Female reproductive health, pregnancy, and childbirth.
covers: Obstetrics, maternal-fetal medicine, gynecology, reproductive endocrinology
Contraception, gynecologic oncology, urogynecology
explicitly_excludes: General care for women → primary care specialties

subspecialties.name: Psychiatry & Mental Health
subspecialties.tagline: Diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of mental and emotional disorders.
covers: Mood disorders, anxiety, psychosis, substance use disorders
Psychopharmacology, psychotherapy, child and adolescent psychiatry

subspecialties.name: Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism
subspecialties.tagline: Hormones, glands, and the metabolic processes they regulate.
covers: Diabetes mellitus, thyroid disease, osteoporosis, pituitary disorders
Metabolic syndrome, adrenal disorders, lipidology

subspecialties.name: Anesthesiology & Pain Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: Perioperative care, sedation, and the management of acute and chronic pain.
covers: General and regional anesthesia, obstetric anesthesia, critical care
Acute and chronic pain management, pharmacology of anesthetics
explicitly_excludes: Management of ICU patients → Pulmonology & Critical Care

subspecialties.name: Pathology & Laboratory Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: The science behind the diagnosis of disease using tissues and fluids.
covers: Anatomic and clinical pathology, histopathology, cytopathology
Laboratory testing, molecular diagnostics, transfusion medicine
explicitly_excludes: Forensic science outside of a clinical context

subspecialties.name: Emergency Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: Care for acute illnesses and injuries requiring immediate attention.
covers: Resuscitation, trauma care, toxicology, disaster medicine
Diagnosis and management of undifferentiated acute conditions
explicitly_excludes: In-patient critical care → Pulmonology & Critical Care

subspecialties.name: Nephrology
subspecialties.tagline: Kidney function, kidney diseases, dialysis, and transplantation.
covers: Chronic kidney disease, acute kidney injury, dialysis, hypertension
Glomerular diseases, electrolyte disorders, renal replacement therapy

subspecialties.name: Dermatology
subspecialties.tagline: Disorders of the skin, hair, and nails.
covers: Psoriasis, eczema, skin cancer, acne, autoimmune skin diseases
Dermatopathology, cosmetic dermatology, dermatologic surgery

subspecialties.name: Rheumatology & Immunology
subspecialties.tagline: Immune system disorders, including arthritis and autoimmune diseases.
covers: Rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, vasculitis, gout, clinical immunology
Allergy, immunodeficiencies, biologic therapies
explicitly_excludes: Osteoarthritis management → Surgery or primary care

subspecialties.name: Urology
subspecialties.tagline: Surgical and medical diseases of the urinary tract and male reproductive system.
covers: Prostate cancer, kidney stones, BPH, incontinence, erectile dysfunction
Urologic oncology, reconstructive urology, endourology

subspecialties.name: Genetics & Genomics
subspecialties.tagline: Inherited disorders and the role of genes in health and disease.
covers: Clinical genetics, genomic medicine, genetic counseling, gene therapy
Diagnosis of rare diseases, pharmacogenomics, cancer genomics

subspecialties.name: Ophthalmology
subspecialties.tagline: Medical and surgical care of the eyes and visual system.
covers: Cataracts, glaucoma, retinal diseases, cornea, neuro-ophthalmology
Refractive surgery, ocular oncology, ophthalmic pathology
explicitly_excludes: Neurological causes of visual disturbance → Neurology
Systemic diseases affecting the eye → relevant medical specialty

subspecialties.name: Otolaryngology (ENT)
subspecialties.tagline: Disorders of the ear, nose, throat, and related head and neck structures.
covers: Sinusitis, hearing loss, sleep apnea, head and neck cancers
Otology, rhinology, laryngology, facial plastic surgery
explicitly_excludes: Brain and spinal cord issues → Neurology or Surgery

uncovered_concerns: Geriatrics was considered but not included as a separate category, as its core literature is often published within organ-specific specialties (e.g., dementia in Neurology journals).
Clinical Trials & Methodology was excluded as a category because trials are best classified by their clinical domain (e.g., a cardiology trial belongs in Cardiology).
Internal Medicine was deemed too broad, functioning as a super-category for many of the chosen subspecialties rather than a distinct field for this level of granularity.
Dentistry & Oral Health was considered but excluded as it is often treated as a separate, parallel field to general medicine with its own distinct literature base.
